div1.entryFree: Brood, subst., the hatch, the young birds hatched at once: doves will peck in safeguard of their b. H6C II, 2, 18. Cor. V, 3, 162. Used of men: bring forth brave b. Tp. III, 2, 113. a b. of traitors, H6B V, 1, 141. Typhon's b. Tit. IV, 2, 94. Tropically: all that b. (of vices) to kill, Lucr. 627. make the earth devour her own b. Sonn. 19, 2. the hatch and b. of time, H4B III, 1, 86. -- To sit on b. == to ponder: there's something in his soul, o'er which his melancholy sits on b. Hml. III, 1, 173.
